title: The possibility of a making of shin eva documentary
display_title: The possibility of a making of shin eva documentary
wikitext:
[]: The possibility of a making of shin eva documentary             Quote Post by Thesufferingpumpkin » Sun Jan 31, 2021 5:54 pm There’s been so much behind the scenes snippets for this movie, more than any behind the scenes of evangelion before it. Does anyone think khara have shot footage for a making of documentary during all these years it’s been in production?

[]: Re: The possibility of a making of shin eva documentary             Quote Post by FXArmaros98 » Sun Jan 31, 2021 6:04 pm Yes It would be really nice for me if maybe they showed us the dubbing sections,interviews with voice actors, even some scenes completely drawn and dubbed but never used and stuff like that.I'd very like it if they showed us some work on this movie's saga.

[]: Re: The possibility of a making of shin eva documentary             Quote Post by Archer » Sun Jan 31, 2021 9:52 pm I'd just like to know what the original plans for Q and Shin were, way early in development back when they were still planned to be a pseudo-double feature like EoE, and how that evolved into the Q and Shin we'll actually be getting. The 2.0 CRC's revealed that the rebuilds originally were just going to be more or less just a straight remake/reboot well into 2.0's development, it would be interesting to see where this new plotline came from and how it's evolved since its inception.

[]: Re: The possibility of a making of shin eva documentary             Quote Post by GhostlyOcam » Sun Jan 31, 2021 9:57 pm I'm not holding on to any hopes for a detailed documentary, however we could get a candid-style behind the scene footages like the one for End of Evangelion . That being said, the production of Rebuild in its entirety itself deserves a feature-length documentary.

[]: Re: The possibility of a making of shin eva documentary             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Mon Feb 01, 2021 12:01 am If I'm not mistaken, the very first talks on the Rebuilds can be traced back to 2002. Anno has been with this series in his mind for almost 20 years by now. There is definitely a bunch of material to create a documentary on it! 5284973 wrote: The 2.0 CRC's revealed that the rebuilds originally were just going to be more or less just a straight remake/reboot well into 2.0's development, it would be interesting to see where this new plotline came from and how it's evolved since its inception.

[]: Re: The possibility of a making of shin eva documentary             Quote Post by GhostlyOcam » Mon Feb 01, 2021 12:25 am WHAT DO YOU MEAN 2002 IS ALMOST 20 YEARS AG- *coughs* Yeah, the production of Rebuild is just as interesting as the films themselves, I'd totally love a feature-length documentary about this. The transition from 2.0 and 3.0/Shin is surely the most desired story everyone wants to know at this point.

[]: Re: The possibility of a making of shin eva documentary             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Mon Feb 01, 2021 1:21 am Yeah, while Anno was starting to think about this film, I was literally being born. Crazy stuff... GhostlyOcam wrote: production of Rebuild is just as interesting as the films themselves

[]: Re: The possibility of a making of shin eva documentary             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Feb 01, 2021 2:04 am I wouldn't expect them to have an all encompassing "We've been filming this the early 2000s" doc but I wouldn't be surprised if they did some kind of doc following the production fo the final film. I've noticed a lot more feature length docs covering productions recently - the excellent Ghibli doc, The Kingdom of Madness and Dreams even has a lot of great Hideaki Anno moments from when he voiced the lead in The Wind Rises - and Khara seems pretty savvy. They've pretty much had people covering every aspect of production - so many tweets - so a doc covering production of Thrice Upon A Time with a lot of early talk about what went on in the making of the first three films doesn't seem outlandish.

[]: Re: The possibility of a making of shin eva documentary             Quote Post by Pluto » Mon Feb 01, 2021 2:19 am I think it would be cool to see the behind the scenes of Anno’s directing process

[]: Re: The possibility of a making of shin eva documentary             Quote Post by Thesufferingpumpkin » Mon Feb 01, 2021 11:47 am It would be a nice money maker for khara to put out a making of doc to be honest

[]: Re: The possibility of a making of shin eva documentary             Quote Post by FXArmaros98 » Mon Feb 01, 2021 11:51 am Thesufferingpumpkin maybe they could sell it as DVD along with a special Blue Ray edition of 3.0 + 1.0

[]: Re: The possibility of a making of shin eva documentary             Quote Post by GhostlyOcam » Tue Feb 02, 2021 11:47 am Gendo'sPapa wrote: They've pretty much had people covering every aspect of production - so many tweets - so a doc covering production of Thrice Upon A Time with a lot of early talk about what went on in the making of the first three films doesn't seem outlandish.

[]: Re: The possibility of a making of shin eva documentary             Quote Post by Joseki » Wed Feb 03, 2021 9:16 am It's not really a "close partnership" when Anno/khara were the founding members.

[]: Re: The possibility of a making of shin eva documentary             Quote Post by GhostlyOcam » Wed Feb 03, 2021 11:37 am Gee, should've known better when I see "anime and tokusatsu", OF COURSE it's Anno, that beautiful 60-years old nerd. In any case, lots of production materials of Rebuild are archived through ATAC so it's only a matter lf time until they see the light of day.
summary:
[]: The possibility of a making of shin eva documentary             Quote Post by Thesufferingpumpkin » Sun Jan 31, 2021 5:54 pm There’s been so much behind the scenes snippets for this movie, more than any behind the scenes of evangelion before it. Does anyone think khara have shot footage for a making of documentary during all these years it’s been in production?

[]: Re: The possibility of a making of shin eva documentary   &nb
